records.rawExplanation: *2021 (multi-year derating starts — partial coverage):* stock ¥110 (Dec 28) – ¥308 (Apr 2) per Nikkei 10-year history; Apr peak → Dec trough ~-64%. FY9/2021 full-year financials not directly surfaced — partial. Stock-move catalyst for the multi-year decline starting here: not specifically sourced — catalyst needs_human (consistent with broader 2021 small-cap growth derating but no name-specific event named). Sign summary: shares fell sharply from ¥308 Apr peak to ¥110 Dec low.

records.rawExplanation: *2022 (stock bottoms, fundamentals turning — partial coverage):* stock ¥84 (Jan 28) – ¥327 (Aug 23); Jan 28 ¥84 was the 5-year low. From the 2021 Apr peak ¥308 to Jan 2022 ¥84 = -73%. Stock recovered to ¥327 by August. FY9/2022 actuals not directly surfaced in snippets. Stock-move catalyst for the recovery off January lows: not directly sourced — catalyst needs_human. Sign summary: shares fell to ¥84 multi-year low (Jan), then recovered to ¥327 high (Aug).

records.rawExplanation: *2023 (multi-year rally begins on real fundamentals):* stock ¥230 (Jan 6) – ¥825 (Sep 7); +3.6x intra-year range. FY9/2023 Q1 (Oct-Dec 2022 release): revenue ¥2,276M (+17.2% YoY), operating profit ¥533M (+9.5%) per company disclosure. Strong start to the fiscal year set the trajectory. Sign summary: revenue rose +17.2% YoY; operating profit rose +9.5%; shares rallied to ¥825 high (Sep 7); business strong, stock rose. 業績 ◎ (Q1 strong start) / 株価 ◎ (3.6x rally off lows).

records.rawExplanation: *2024 (record-year confirmation — Q1 Compounder):* stock ¥421 (Mar 14) – ¥1,039 (Dec 16); broke ¥1,000 for the first time. **FY9/2024 actual (announced Nov 14, 2024): revenue grew +20.5% YoY, operating profit ¥2.0B** per Nikkei disclosure + IR materials. Continued strong fundamental growth. Sign summary: revenue rose +20.5% YoY; operating profit rose strongly; record results; shares rallied to ¥1,039 high (Dec 16); business strong, stock strong. 業績 ◎ / 株価 ◎ (Q1 Compounder).

records.name: Sharingtechnology
records.rawExplanation:
*2025 (record-year #2 — clean ◎):* stock ¥813 (Jan 10) – ¥1,262 (Aug 14); new multi-year high ¥1,262. **FY9/2025 actual (announced Nov 14, 2025): revenue ¥8,580M (+14.4% YoY), operating profit ¥2,074M (+15.9%)** — 2-year-highest profit, **2nd consecutive year of record results**, **annual dividend lifted ¥15** (capital return signal — alpha-menu U67 territory) per kabutan + IR disclosure. Sign summary: revenue rose +14.4% YoY; operating profit rose +15.9%; record results; profit margin expanding; dividend hike of ¥15; shares rallied to ¥1,262 multi-year high (Aug 14); business strong, stock strong. 業績 ◎ / 株価 ◎ (Q1 Compounder, third year of the rally).

Cautious side: stock has rallied ~15x off the Jan 2022 ¥84 low — valuation has run substantially; the underlying business is genuinely growing but at +14-20% revenue, not at the rate the stock has appreciated; multi-year compounder story is real but stock multiple is now elevated; market for "home-trouble platform" services is steady but not infinite — TAM cap matters.

Sources:
- https://www.sharing-tech.co.jp/_ir/pdf/2025_Q4_1/results.pdf (FY9/2025 通期決算短信 IFRS, Nov 14, 2025)
- https://kabutan.jp/news/?b=k202511140421 (FY9/2025 record profit + dividend hike summary)
- https://www.nikkei.com/nkd/disclosure/tdnr/20241114523234/ (FY9/2024 通期決算説明資料)
- https://www.nikkei.com/nkd/disclosure/tdnr/20241114523182/ (FY9/2024 通期決算短信 IFRS)
- https://www.sharing-tech.co.jp/_ir/pdf/2025_Q4_1/presentation.pdf (FY9/2025 決算説明資料 + 成長可能性)
- https://www.buffett-code.com/company/3989/ (company profile + segments)
- https://www.nikkei.com/nkd/company/history/yprice/?scode=3989 (Nikkei 10-year price history — all per-year highs/lows)
records.bearCase: stock has rallied ~15x off the Jan 2022 ¥84 low — valuation has run substantially; the underlying business is genuinely growing but at +14-20% revenue, not at the rate the stock has appreciated; multi-year compounder story is real but stock multiple is now elevated; market for "home-trouble platform" services is steady but not infinite — TAM cap matters
